prompts.title: Cabinet Note Drafting Framework
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior IAS officer (Joint Secretary level) with 18 years across Indian central ministries. Draft a Cabinet Note for an inter-ministerial proposal I will paste. Use the standard GoI format: (1) subject, (2) background, (3) issue + reason for note, (4) options analyzed (3 minimum) with pros/cons + financial + legal + admin implications, (5) inter-ministerial consultation status (with departments named), (6) PMO views (if any), (7) recommendation with explicit basis, (8) draft Cabinet decision. Constraints: align with Allocation of Business Rules + Transaction of Business Rules; cite enabling Act + Section; INR figures in lakh/crore; 8-page maximum; avoid bureaucratic clichés.

prompts.title: RTI Application Response Letter
prompts.prompt: Act as a Public Information Officer with 8 years handling RTI applications under the RTI Act 2005. Draft a 350-word response to an RTI seeking information on departmental tendering decisions. Cover: (1) acknowledgment + reference number, (2) information provided per Section 4, (3) information withheld with specific Section 8(1) clause cited, (4) third-party consultation done (where applicable), (5) appeal rights (FAA + CIC, 30 days + 90 days timelines), (6) fee adjustment, (7) signed by PIO with designation. Constraints: align with RTI Act 2005 + DoPT guidelines; never reflexively withhold; protect personal info per Section 8(1)(j); CIC-defensible reasoning.

prompts.title: District Annual Plan Outline
prompts.prompt: Act as a District Magistrate with 12 years across UP, MP, and Bihar. Build a comprehensive District Annual Plan for FY 2026-27 for a 35-lakh-population district. Plan should cover: (1) demographic snapshot, (2) sector-wise priorities (health, education, agriculture, rural development, urban, women & child, scheduled caste welfare, infrastructure, law & order), (3) flagship scheme alignment (PMJAY, PMAY, Jal Jeevan, MGNREGA, PMGSY, ICDS, PMKVY), (4) budget allocation per sector, (5) outcome KPIs per sector, (6) inter-departmental coordination matrix, (7) review mechanism (quarterly DCC), (8) people's plan integration. Constraints: align with NITI Aayog DAP template; INR in crores; convergence-led; SDG-mapped.

prompts.title: Public Address Speech for Republic Day
prompts.prompt: Act as a District Magistrate addressing 5,000 citizens at the District HQ Republic Day function. Write a 12-minute speech (~1,500 words) covering: (1) salutation + national pride, (2) constitutional values, (3) district achievements (3-5 highlights with numbers), (4) ongoing flagship initiatives, (5) call to citizens (specific civic action), (6) tribute to district's freedom-fighters (1 named locally), (7) closing pledge. Constraints: bilingual (Hindi 80%, English 20%); honour cultural sentiments; flag-code compliant; no political party mentioned; under 12 minutes; ends with Jai Hind.

prompts.title: Inter-Departmental Coordination Memo
prompts.prompt: Act as a Secretary (Forests) coordinating with Secretary (Tribal Welfare) on a Forest Rights Act implementation issue. Draft a 700-word inter-dept memo. Cover: (1) issue context (Section 3 forest dwellers' rights pending claims), (2) bottleneck (claims rejection rate 38%, escalation backlog), (3) proposed joint approach (3 actions), (4) responsibilities matrix (RACI), (5) timeline (90 days), (6) budget split, (7) joint review meeting (monthly), (8) escalation to CM if not resolved. Constraints: align with FRA 2006 + Rules; respect tribal community sensitivity; cite Section 5 Gram Sabha process; offer DM + tribal welfare DM as nodal officers.

prompts.title: Disaster Response After-Action Review
prompts.prompt: Act as a District Collector who managed a major flood (50K affected, 12 fatalities, 2-week response). Write a 1,400-word after-action review. Cover: (1) timeline of events + decisions, (2) response phases (rescue, relief, recovery), (3) what worked (3 with metrics), (4) what didn't (3 honestly), (5) coordination with NDRF + SDRF + State + Centre, (6) communication (citizens, media, leadership), (7) financial response (₹ Cr from SDRF + DM contingency), (8) recommendations for SOP update, (9) team commendations, (10) follow-up commitments (rehabilitation, infrastructure rebuild). Constraints: align with NDMA framework + state DM Act; honest not defensive; preserve dignity of victims; data-anchored.

prompts.title: Vigilance Inquiry Closure Report
prompts.prompt: Act as a Vigilance Officer with 10 years across CBI / state vigilance. Draft a 1,000-word vigilance inquiry closure report on a procurement irregularity allegation. Cover: (1) allegation summary, (2) preliminary inquiry findings, (3) records examined (file notings, tender, financial trail), (4) statements recorded (3 officers + vendor), (5) factual findings, (6) violations identified (CCS Conduct Rules, GFR), (7) recommendation (no-action / minor penalty / major penalty / criminal referral to CBI), (8) reasoning + precedent cited, (9) sign-off + onward submission. Constraints: align with CVC Manual + AIS Conduct Rules; principles of natural justice (charged officer's reply heard); CBI-handover threshold respected.

prompts.title: Press Release on Flagship Scheme Launch
prompts.prompt: Act as a District Information Officer with 7 years of PIB-style press releases. Write a 350-word press release announcing the district launch of PM Surya Ghar Yojana (rooftop solar). Cover: (1) headline + dateline, (2) opening (event + chief guest), (3) scheme key features (subsidy, eligibility, application), (4) district targets (5,000 households in year 1), (5) channels for citizens (CSC, online, district kiosk), (6) DM quote + technical-team quote, (7) supporting data, (8) sign-off + media contact. Constraints: align with PIB format; bilingual (Hindi + English); avoid political branding; embargo until launch event; mention MEDA / SECI as implementing partner.

prompts.title: Section 144 CrPC Order Drafting
prompts.prompt: Act as a Sub-Divisional Magistrate (SDM) with judicial-magistrate powers. Draft a Section 144 CrPC order (now BNSS Section 163) prohibiting public assembly in 4 specified police-station limits during communal tension. Cover: (1) preamble (basis of jurisdiction), (2) factual situation (intelligence inputs, FIRs, prior incidents), (3) urgency justification (public peace, life and property), (4) prohibited activities (assembly of >5 persons, weapons, loudspeakers, social-media inciting posts, processions), (5) duration (60 days, with 14-day review), (6) exempt categories, (7) violation consequences, (8) appeal forum + review, (9) signed + dated. Constraints: align with BNSS 2023 (replacing CrPC 1973); proportionality + least-restrictive principle; cite SC judgments (Anuradha Bhasin, Ramlila Maidan); avoid blanket internet shutdown.

prompts.title: Annual Report Section for State Department
prompts.prompt: Act as a Director (Higher Education) at a state-government department. Write the annual report section (1,800 words) for the department's 2025-26 performance. Cover: (1) sectoral overview, (2) major achievements (5 with metrics), (3) flagship schemes (state + central), (4) key statistics (enrolment, GER, faculty, infrastructure), (5) initiatives launched (3 new), (6) collaborations (universities, AICTE, UGC, NAAC), (7) challenges + responses, (8) financial performance (₹ Cr utilisation), (9) plans for 2026-27, (10) photos / infographics suggestions. Constraints: align with state government annual report template; SDG-mapped; gender + caste disaggregation; avoid achievement inflation.

prompts.title: Confidential Report Writing on Subordinate
prompts.prompt: Act as a District Magistrate writing the Confidential Report (CR / APAR) for a Tehsildar. Cover: (1) integrity (no doubt / no specific finding), (2) work performance assessment (8 dimensions: planning, execution, supervision, public dealing, target achievement, team management, communication, decision-making), (3) attributes (initiative, judgment, conduct), (4) training needs identified, (5) suitability for specific assignments (3 named), (6) overall grade (Outstanding / Very Good / Good / Average / Below Average) with justification, (7) reviewing officer column (left blank for next-up). Constraints: align with AIS rules + DoPT APAR guidelines; balanced (not all 'outstanding'); evidence-cited not vibes; observe natural justice (subject can rebut).

prompts.title: Citizen Charter Revamp Document
prompts.prompt: Act as a Joint Secretary (Reforms) with 9 years of citizen-charter implementation. Build the revamped Citizen Charter for the District Municipal Corporation. Cover: (1) vision + mission, (2) 18 services rendered with: (a) timeline commitment (in days/hours), (b) documents required, (c) fee, (d) escalation officer + contact, (e) penalty for delay (compensation), (3) grievance redress mechanism + appeal, (4) MIS dashboard (publicly visible), (5) feedback survey integration, (6) review cycle, (7) public-disclosure protocol. Constraints: align with Sevottam framework; bilingual; outcome-oriented language; mention PG portal + RTI fallback; printable format + website-ready.

prompts.title: Police Station-Level Crime Review Memo
prompts.prompt: Act as an SP (Superintendent of Police) writing the monthly crime review memo for a police station with rising property-crime trend. Cover: (1) crime statistics MoM + YoY (IPC categories), (2) hot-spot mapping (3 wards), (3) modus operandi pattern, (4) detection rate + chargesheeting performance, (5) preventive measures (beat patrolling, CCTV expansion, Mohalla committee), (6) action against habitual offenders (preventive arrests under Sections of BNSS), (7) station-level officer training needs, (8) resource ask from district HQ, (9) one-line directive to SHO. Constraints: align with NCRB classification + state PHQ format; cite Bhartiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita 2023 (BNSS); fair to officers (not blame culture); evidence-led not anecdotal.

prompts.title: Procurement Tender Document Outline
prompts.prompt: Act as a Tender Officer with 11 years of GeM + e-tender procurement. Draft a tender document for a ₹3.2 Cr school furniture supply across 240 government schools. Cover: (1) NIT (notice inviting tender) on GeM, (2) eligibility criteria (annual turnover ₹6+ Cr, GST registration, prior similar work), (3) technical specs (BIS-compliant, ergonomic, IS-codes), (4) bid evaluation criteria (L1 vs QCBS), (5) timeline (publish, pre-bid, submit, opening, award, supply), (6) MSME + SC/ST vendor preference per Rule 153, (7) performance security (3%), (8) liquidated damages, (9) grievance redress, (10) draft contract. Constraints: align with GFR 2017 + Make-in-India + GeM rules; transparency above ₹5L; avoid favouring brand-specific specs.

prompts.title: Note for Council of Ministers (State Cabinet)
prompts.prompt: Act as Principal Secretary (Health) drafting a State Cabinet Note on universal health-insurance scheme integration with PMJAY. Cover: (1) issue brief, (2) background + national policy direction, (3) state-specific context + gaps, (4) options (4 with cost-benefit), (5) financial implications (5-year + INR Cr), (6) administrative + legal implications, (7) consultation done (Health, Finance, Welfare, NHM), (8) recommendation + draft Cabinet decision, (9) implementation roadmap (90 days). Constraints: align with state Cabinet Secretariat format; cite Ayushman Bharat + PMJAY scheme guidelines; respect state-Centre fiscal balance; avoid party-political language.

prompts.title: Budget Speech Section for State Finance Minister
prompts.prompt: Act as a Joint Secretary (Finance) writing the agriculture-section of the State Finance Minister's annual budget speech. ~700 words. Cover: (1) sector context + farmer welfare priority, (2) 5 specific announcements (allocation + scheme + outcome), (3) capital expenditure for irrigation, (4) credit access (Kisan Credit Card linkage), (5) MSP + market linkage, (6) climate resilience (drought, micro-irrigation), (7) FPO promotion, (8) farmer income doubling commitment, (9) numerical commitments (INR Cr) + target beneficiary count. Constraints: align with state budget speech tradition; bilingual phrases (Hindi or regional + English); cite Centre's parallel schemes; defensible numbers; simple language.

prompts.title: Public Grievance Redress SOP
prompts.prompt: Act as a Director (Grievance Redress) at a Central Ministry. Draft an SOP for handling public grievances received via CPGRAMS portal + Twitter/X public feedback. Cover: (1) grievance intake (auto-classify by ministry/department/sub-department), (2) acknowledgment timeline (24 hrs), (3) preliminary investigation (3 days), (4) escalation matrix (T1 dealing-officer → T2 desk officer → T3 deputy secretary → T4 joint secretary), (5) target resolution time (15 days), (6) compensation if delayed (per Sevottam), (7) closure process + appeal, (8) MIS dashboard + monthly report to Cabinet, (9) X-post-handling protocol. Constraints: align with DARPG + CPGRAMS; integrate IVR + CSC + WhatsApp channels; data-driven not box-ticking; respond in citizen's language.

prompts.title: Mid-Career LBSNAA Training Module Brief
prompts.prompt: Act as a Senior Director at LBSNAA designing a 4-week module for mid-career IAS officers (Phase III, batch 2014). Topic: 'Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) for District Administration'. Brief should cover: (1) learning objectives (5), (2) module agenda (Day 1-20 with topics), (3) faculty mix (academia, practitioners, IndiaStack architects), (4) field visit (UIDAI Bengaluru, NPCI, DigiLocker, NIC), (5) case-study basis (Aadhaar, UPI, ONDC, e-KYC, OCEN), (6) hands-on lab (DigiLocker integration, UPI BBPS demo), (7) project assignment (DPI roadmap for own district), (8) assessment, (9) post-training cohort (DPI-leaders network). Constraints: align with DOPT mid-career training framework; balance theory + field; respect officers' seniority; produce district-level deployment roadmaps.
